goals.g1: Wire compute_unchanged_candidates into reverse_footprint_lightweight, replacing the coarse over-approximation (all touched positions) with the precise unchanged/changed distinction: a candidate node is excluded from S_reverse_implied only if it is CONFIRMED unchanged for this specific firing (candidate per compute_unchanged_candidates AND its confirmed crossings equal its declared crossings via _read_boundary_externals); everything else counts as changed.

prep_note: Closes the imprecision flagged in ers_reverse_fire_implemented.json. Prepared before implementing.

prior_candidates.statement: Two-part precomputation, correctly scoped: (1) STATIC, schema-level, computed once per rule (new function compute_unchanged_candidates(graph2)): a node is a CANDIDATE-unchanged node iff (a) bijectively mapped (out_node in core.inherit -- already guaranteed injective, no new work) AND (b) its declared spectrum matches: internal-edges-touching-in_node union expected_crossing-categories-touching-in_node (input side) EQUALS internal-edges-touching-out_node union boundary_grab-categories-touching-out_node (output side). (2) PER-FIRING, using the already-built _read_boundary_externals: a candidate node is CONFIRMED-unchanged for a specific firing iff its confirmed crossings (exists AND declared) exactly match its declared crossings (i.e. nothing was merely-declared-but-absent for this firing, closing the ph_all_four-style gap). (3) CONNECTIVITY: confirmed-unchanged nodes connected to other confirmed-unchanged nodes extend the anchor region transitively -- flood-fill outward from any confirmed-unchanged node through edges to other confirmed-unchanged nodes.

derived_from.rule: combine the free static part (bijective, already computed) with a new static spectrum-match narrowing, and require per-firing confirmation to close the declared-vs-actual gap already found this session

goals: Determine whether SchemaCore/compile_core could precompute, statically from graph2, which nodes are bijectively mapped (clean 1:1 inherit, no fan-in/fan-out), have an unchanged edge spectrum (same internal+crossing (type,direction) categories before/after), and are transitively connected to other such nodes -- to replace reverse_footprint_lightweight's coarse over-approximation with a precise, schema-level unchanged/changed distinction.

derived_from.rule: combine the free static part (bijective, already computed) with a new static spectrum-match narrowing, and require per-firing confirmation to close the declared-vs-actual gap already found this session (imported, parents not re-grounded — external provenance)

goals: Record the compute_unchanged_candidates implementation and its two-case verification (spectrum mismatch correctly excluded for ph_all_four; spectrum match correctly included for the explicit single-category rule).

prior_candidates.statement: Propagating 'unchanged' status to nodes connected to other confirmed-unchanged nodes is exactly the ANCHOR mechanism's original intent (ers_reversibility_anchor: unchanged nodes as known-identity anchors, growing correspondence outward) -- but built on a PRECISE, schema-derivable foundation (bijective + matching declared spectrum) instead of the coarse over-approximation in reverse_footprint_lightweight (which currently treats ALL touched positions as equally 'changed', explicitly flagged as capable of false mismatches).
goals: Determine whether SchemaCore/compile_core could precompute, statically from graph2, which nodes are bijectively mapped (clean 1:1 inherit, no fan-in/fan-out), have an unchanged edge spectrum (same internal+crossing (type,direction) categories before/after), and are transitively connected to other such nodes -- to replace reverse_footprint_lightweight's coarse over-approximation with a precise, schema-level unchanged/changed distinction.

prior_candidates.statement: Propagating 'unchanged' status to nodes connected to other confirmed-unchanged nodes is exactly the ANCHOR mechanism's original intent (ers_reversibility_anchor: unchanged nodes as known-identity anchors, growing correspondence outward) -- but built on a PRECISE, schema-derivable foundation (bijective + matching declared spectrum) instead of the coarse over-approximation in reverse_footprint_lightweight (which currently treats ALL touched positions as equally 'changed', explicitly flagged as capable of false mismatches).
goals: Record the compute_unchanged_candidates implementation and its two-case verification (spectrum mismatch correctly excluded for ph_all_four; spectrum match correctly included for the explicit single-category rule).

prior_candidates.statement: build_reversed_operation should become PER-BINDING, not per-rule: instead of build_reversed_graph2 constructing new-input categories from the blanket boundary_grab declaration (set(preserve.get(old_out))), it should use confirmed = _read_boundary_externals(operation.schema.edits, binding, base_graph) for the SPECIFIC firing being checked, and derive new-input categories from confirmed[old_out]'s actual (type,direction) pairs only -- a direct, per-firing exchange of the ALREADY-VERIFIED crossings, not a blanket requirement of everything merely declared possible.

derived_from.rule: the mechanism computing the AND's confirmed side already exists (forward direction); reuse it directly to build the per-firing reversed pattern instead of the blanket declared set

goals: Fix the ph_all_four zero-match bug using Sven's physical-switch idea: when a crossing's existence AND declaration both hold (confirmed per the actual firing via _read_boundary_externals), directly exchange the placeholder mapping between the input and mapped-output sides, per binding -- rather than requiring a fixed, static, blanket set of categories (all 4 of ph_all_four) that may not all be present in any given real firing.

current_lightweight_code.statement: reverse_footprint_lightweight (reverse_compound.py, current code): for each inherit pair, unconditionally adds binding[in_node] to the footprint -- ALL survived positions, no unchanged/changed distinction. Confirmed by direct code read.

wiring_design.statement: reverse_footprint_lightweight is revised: for each reversed match's core.inherit pairs, a survived position (binding[in_node]) is CONFIRMED unchanged and EXCLUDED from the footprint iff (a) (in_node,out_node) is in compute_unchanged_candidates(reversed_op.graph2) AND (b) confirmed = _read_boundary_externals(reversed_op.schema.edits, binding, result_graph) for this node equals its DECLARED crossings exactly (nothing merely-declared-but-absent). All born positions remain unconditionally in the footprint. Any survived position failing (a) or (b) is included (conservative fallback, same as before for anything not confirmed-unchanged).

derived_from.rule: replace the all-survived-positions-included coarse rule with a precise exclusion only for confirmed-unchanged candidates, keeping the same conservative fallback for everything else

bug_found_and_fixed.statement: compute_unchanged_candidates, in actual use, caught a real separate bug in build_reversed_graph2: old input_nodes' OWN internal structure (e.g. A's self-loop encoding bit value 1) was never copied to the new output side -- only old output_nodes' internal_edges were copied to the new input side. Confirmed by direct inspection: reversed graph2's new-output node lacked a self-loop that should have been there. Fixed by adding a symmetric step using the already-built _input_internal_edges helper (the same one compute_unchanged_candidates itself uses).

wiring_tested.statement: After the fix, re-tested both toy rules (explicit single-category and ph_all_four). Both now correctly report s_reverse_implied as EMPTY (confirmed unchanged) instead of the coarse {real_A}. Verified this is CORRECT, not a new bug: both toy rules perform an identical, trivial no-op-like transformation on real_A (self-loop + one preserved crossing, literally identical incident sets before/after in this test), and once the physical-switch fix narrows the reversed pattern to CONFIRMED categories at construction time, the ph_all_four vs explicit declaration-style distinction stops mattering for this check -- both correctly resolve to unchanged. This is a coherent, validating outcome: it shows the physical-switch fix and compute_unchanged_candidates compose correctly, not that the check has become vacuous.

derived_from.rule: verify a surprising result against the actual ground truth (are the real incident sets literally identical in this test?) before accepting or rejecting it as correct

chk_falsifier_wiring_tested.method: could this new construction (per-binding confirmed_crossings during build_reversed_operation, THEN a separate confirmed-unchanged check in reverse_footprint_lightweight) be masking a genuine difference by narrowing too aggressively at BOTH stages redundantly, making the check unable to ever find a genuine mismatch?
chk_falsifier_wiring_tested.result: No: the two checks operate on different things -- build_reversed_operation's confirmed_crossings narrows the PATTERN'S requirements to what was true in the ORIGINAL forward firing (a one-time construction fact); reverse_footprint_lightweight's check verifies the ACTUAL match found by blind search (collect_reversed_matches) against its OWN confirmed crossings for that specific match -- these could still diverge if match_all found a match to a DIFFERENT region with different real structure, which would surface as a genuine mismatch there. Not proven un-maskable in general, but not shown vacuous by this test either.

side_findings.finding: whether the two-stage confirmed-crossing narrowing could ever mask a genuine mismatch in a more complex case (not this trivial toy rule) is untested

chk_relevance_wiring_tested.method: connect wiring_tested's evidence to this session's stated goal
chk_relevance_wiring_tested.result: Goal: wire compute_unchanged_candidates into reverse_footprint_lightweight for a precise footprint. Directly reports the implementation, the bug found and fixed along the way, and verified test results.

committed.statement: After the fix, re-tested both toy rules (explicit single-category and ph_all_four). Both now correctly report s_reverse_implied as EMPTY (confirmed unchanged) instead of the coarse {real_A}. Verified this is CORRECT, not a new bug: both toy rules perform an identical, trivial no-op-like transformation on real_A (self-loop + one preserved crossing, literally identical incident sets before/after in this test), and once the physical-switch fix narrows the reversed pattern to CONFIRMED categories at construction time, the ph_all_four vs explicit declaration-style distinction stops mattering for this check -- both correctly resolve to unchanged. This is a coherent, validating outcome: it shows the physical-switch fix and compute_unchanged_candidates compose correctly, not that the check has become vacuous.
